title: TypeScript for Devs
description: Move from JavaScript to TypeScript with confidence.

lessons.title: Typed Variables
lessons.subtitle: Primitive annotations
lessons.starterCode: // TODO: declare name: string = 'Ada' and age: number = 36, then log them
lessons.task:
Declare two typed variables and log them on separate lines. Expected output:
Ada
36
lessons.hint: let name: string = 'Ada'; let age: number = 36;

lessons.title: Function Signatures
lessons.subtitle: Typed parameters and return
lessons.starterCode: // TODO: write add(a: number, b: number): number and call it with 2, 3
lessons.task: Implement and call add. Must print 5.
lessons.hint: function add(a: number, b: number): number { return a + b; }

lessons.title: Interfaces
lessons.subtitle: Object shapes
lessons.starterCode: // TODO: define interface User { name: string; age: number } and log u.name
lessons.task: Declare a User interface, create u: User, and log the name. Expected: Linus
lessons.hint: interface User { name: string; age: number } const u: User = { name: 'Linus', age: 54 };

lessons.title: Generics
lessons.subtitle: Reusable type parameters
lessons.starterCode: // TODO: function first<T>(arr: T[]): T | undefined — log first([10,20,30])
lessons.task: Implement and call a generic first(). Must print 10.
lessons.hint: function first<T>(arr: T[]): T | undefined { return arr[0]; }

lessons.title: Union Types
lessons.subtitle: Narrowing with typeof
lessons.starterCode: // TODO: function fmt(v: string | number): string — log fmt(42) and fmt('hi')
lessons.task: fmt(42) should log 'n:42' and fmt('hi') should log 's:hi'.
